item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, thank you for reading. I did side-step using the actual word "ghost", but I figured there wasn't much point in beating around the bush with regards to what he is.

The dates were the same. The inscription, particularly "Greatest Cave Explorer Ever Known" is both what's on the real-life Floyd Collins' tombstone and what in-story Floyd mentioned briefly in his journal ("I know what I want on my grave, I only worry I will never have the opportunity, now.").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While out-right stating "SCP-3572-1 is a ghost" made me a bit wary going into this, the journal entries and the eerie mystery of the cave made up for it. Gave a good Ted the Caver vibe.

Quick question: was Floyd's inscription on the totem pole the same one that ends up on his grave?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, thank you for reading.

I'm not the best at coming up with inherently original anomalies. This one was mostly about the historical story and how it could have taken place in the Foundation universe.

That said, there is more stuff going on in the "background" of the SCP. Stuff about what 3572 actually is and how it works (and the big ol' totem pole in the cave), but that all seemed more fitting for tales than the actual article.

At some point after the holidays I'll go over it with a fine tooth comb again for errors. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I’ll say this isn’t the most intriguing anomaly, that it’s a bit long for the amount of provided intrigue, and the whole “ghost that roams these caves” bit is a tad cliche, but the article is engaging in its prose to me (I wanted to finish it), it gives us a really layered support for the cliche, is based on some real-life stuff, and I found myself thinking about this article fondly several days after I read it.

So all that indicates to me this is worthy of a +1, even if there are some intangibles as to my reasoning why.

Edit: I have a real fear of spelunking and falling down crevices in caves. Maybe that’s all that’s going on on my end here. But I feel like there’s more.

Also, I would’ve normally kept track of things like this to change them for you, but I know there were some syntactical and grammatical errors in this. Not many.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Floyd is conceptually bound, so he can appear in any form of cave. He's just most frequently sighted in Mammoth cave.

Good catch on the hair though I'll fix that when I'm home. Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It says in the Vietnamese police report:

He was black-haired

but then a few lines later it says

The proprietors have promised to watch for a blond American

Overall, pretty good, though it's unclear if the cave in Kentucky is linked to the one in Vietnam, in which case the bit about "only accessible through a singular surface entry" doesn't make sense.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that one is intentional. I thought about putting in a [Sic] from the Foundation but I thought if I put that one in I'd kind of be obligated to put it on the capitalized segments and the outdated spellings too, so I left it off.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
(This is located in the second addendum)

January 30th, 1925

I have Failed and I will likely die here

Is 'Failed' supposed to be capitalized in that entry?
Aside from a few things (such as missing the Item #: SCP-3572 and Object Class: Euclid), it looks pretty good to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The photo still has SCP-XXXX as its caption

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Down in Mammoth Cave, is where his body laid,
The walls caved in, life could not be saved,
No man-made machine could see the things he'd seen,
Mr. Collins, you did not die in vain!
Black Stone Cherry - Ghost of Floyd Collins

Big heckin' scip here. One of the longer things I've ever written. Original draft was last Decemberish and I got a wild hair to go back over it this last week. It might still be a bit unwieldy, but this also feels like the best I can get it. Hope you all like it.

Thanks to Hippo (a year ago!) and BlueJones for giving me crit on the IRC. Hippo also gave me a much better idea for how to handle Floyd. Thanks also to my real-life buddies Carl and Rasheab for proofreading and feedback too.

The image of Sand Cave was borrowed from:

Original Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Sand_Cave.jpg
Creator: Nicholas Frost

(The picture of Floyd has been reprinted in just about every book and article on the subject and I can't track down the original photographer. As near as I can tell it's public domain since it's 90+ years old at this point. I'll remove it if there's any evidence to the contrary.)
